fields.description:
# Add focused prerequisite consistency review

## Goal
Evaluate whether a ticket plan remains consistent with each direct prerequisite without injecting prerequisite material into general reviewers or changing existing parent and child review behavior.

## Upstream contract
This story implements against the certified material-pin interface from `75de-02b0-d454-410b` and the certified review-phase interface from `5967-4476-7f9f-442d`. The pin interface supplies one review-time `PlanRelationSnapshot` preload keyed by authoritative canonical ticket ID, including relation kind, readable state, pinned material, and ticket plan text. This story neither recollects relations nor changes parent and child context handling.

## Implementation
- Register one prerequisite-consistency criterion. It runs only when the shared `PlanRelationSnapshot` preload succeeded and contains readable direct `depends_on` or inverse `blocks` prerequisites. The same canonical ID reached through both relation forms is evaluated once.
- Extend the production Pass-1 batch runner with a prerequisite-only route. It receives the subject plan plus stable-order whole prerequisite blocks from the preload, packs as many blocks as fit, and invokes the dedicated `plan_review_prerequisite_coverage` structured-output contract. General criterion requests receive only their existing subject and criterion inputs.
- Define measurable isolation: capture every emitted request and assert that each general finder, general verifier, and coach system prompt, user prompt, and instructions contain neither prerequisite canonical IDs nor prerequisite plan text. Also assert exactly one relation preload supplies both pin creation and prerequisite routing. Only the two focused prerequisite prompts may receive prerequisite material.
- Add `_prerequisite_coverage_model()` in `passes.py` and register `contracts.register_contract("plan_review_prerequisite_coverage", _prerequisite_coverage_model)`. Its output is `records: list[PrerequisiteCoverageRecord]`. Each record is `{prerequisite_id: canonical string, disposition: consistent|finding|indeterminate, findings: list[Finding], reason_code?: evaluation-error|output-invalid|attribution-invalid, detail?: string}`.
- Require exactly one record per readable pinned canonical ID. `consistent` requires no findings or reason code. `finding` requires at least one finding and no reason code, with every finding carrying that same canonical `prerequisite_id`. `indeterminate` requires no findings and one closed-enum reason code; detail is optional and non-authoritative. Missing, duplicate, unknown-ID, parse-invalid, or schema-invalid output maps every unresolved ID in the affected bin to `output-invalid`. Provider or model failure maps unresolved IDs to `evaluation-error`. Indeterminate records never assert incompatibility.
- Keep batching semantically neutral. Every focused Pass-1 prompt asks for independent subject-to-one-prerequisite judgments and forbids cross-prerequisite findings. Stable bins contain whole blocks only; neither the subject nor a prerequisite is split. A conflict in the last stable-order block is evaluated identically to a singleton call.
- Add a focused Pass-2 route using prompt `plan-review-prerequisite-verifier` and contract `plan_review_prerequisite_verification`. For every Pass-1 `finding` record, the runner supplies the subject plan, the authoritative prerequisite ID and plan block looked up from the preload, and only that records findings. Multiple independent record inputs may share a call when whole record blocks fit the context budget. The verifier output is indexed by authoritative prerequisite ID and finding index; it cannot select or rewrite the expected ID.
- Define `_prerequisite_verification_model()` in `passes.py` and register it. Its `PrerequisiteVerificationBinary` contains only `prerequisite_attribution_valid=yes|no|insufficient`. `yes` means the finding is causally about incompatibility between the subject and the supplied authoritative prerequisite block; `no` means it belongs to another prerequisite or lacks support in that pair; `insufficient` cannot uphold the finding. This field is a veto and never contributes to validity arithmetic. General `Binary`, `_BINARY_NA_DEFAULT`, `GRADED_BINARY`, and the general verifier contract remain unchanged.
- Merge focused Pass-2 results into the existing Pass-3 path. An upheld prerequisite finding must have `prerequisite_attribution_valid=yes`; `no`, `insufficient`, missing, duplicate, malformed, wrong-ID, or out-of-range verification converts that prerequisites coverage record to `indeterminate/attribution-invalid` and removes its incompatibility findings. Reuse the phase story threshold function for otherwise valid focused findings; do not add a second decide policy.
- Size the actual focused prompt topology, including system prompt, subject plan, prerequisite or record blocks, output reserve, and headroom, through a prerequisite-specific adapter in `sizing.py`. On a context-limit error, split a stable-order bin into contiguous halves recursively. Escalate a singleton through `models_at_or_above()`. Exhaustion at the largest window emits `indeterminate/evaluation-error` with `detail=input-too-large`. Non-context provider failure emits `indeterminate/evaluation-error` for every unresolved ID in that bin.
- Extend `plan_review_decide_input.schema.json` with required-on-focused-route `prerequisite_coverage` and `prerequisite_verifications` arrays and extend the corresponding decide output and coach input schemas. Wire those arrays through both agentic and single-turn branches in `plan-review.yaml`. `plan_review_decide` validates coverage and focused-verification completeness before partitioning, removes unsupported findings, and returns the final coverage records.
- Thread final prerequisite coverage from both decide branches into `plan_review_coach`. The coach sets `coverage.prerequisite_indeterminate=true` when any record is indeterminate. In `orchestrator.finalize_verdict`, test this flag before the existing BLOCK and PASS branches and force top-level `INDETERMINATE`. The signing boundary in `plan_review.__init__` signs only PASS, so this verdict is necessarily unsigned and cannot be certified.
- Add optional `gold_prerequisite_id` and `pred_prerequisite_id` fields to `parity.ItemRecord`, both defaulting to `None`. Preserve every existing field, `CONTAINER_CRITERIA`, `attribution_accuracy`, `packing_spot_eval`, and `container_fidelity_report` behavior. Add a separate `prerequisite_fidelity_report` that reuses the general recall and false-acceptance calculations and separately scores authoritative-ID attribution with the two new fields. Existing container gold-corpus report values and serialized report output remain unchanged.
- Add a separate opt-in `prerequisite_packing_spot_eval` in `fidelity_spot_eval.py`; do not extend or route prerequisite data through `packing_spot_eval` or `container_fidelity_report`. Add at least eight gold sets of at least three prerequisites: two all-consistent, two single-conflict, two tail-conflict where the conflict is last in stable bin order, one attribution-confusion, and one multi-bin case. Compare packed calls with singleton calls. Packed finding recall may not decrease, false acceptance may not increase, every readable ID must have one coverage record, and corpus-wide prerequisite attribution errors must be zero. Failure retains smaller bins or singleton calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] General finder, general verifier, and coach requests contain no prerequisite canonical IDs or plan text.
- [ ] One shared relation preload supplies both material-pin creation and prerequisite routing.
- [ ] Existing parent and child criteria produce the same findings and verdicts on the existing container gold corpus.
- [ ] Stable focused batching never splits the subject or a prerequisite block.
- [ ] Every readable pinned prerequisite receives exactly one closed-schema coverage disposition.
- [ ] A focused Pass-2 verification receives the authoritative plan block for exactly the prerequisite ID it verifies.
- [ ] Missing or incorrect canonical attribution cannot produce a blocking finding.
- [ ] Every upheld prerequisite finding carries its Pass-2-verified authoritative prerequisite ID.
- [ ] Every indeterminate record uses the closed reason-code enum and contains no incompatibility finding.
- [ ] Any indeterminate prerequisite record forces a top-level unsigned INDETERMINATE verdict even when other findings would BLOCK or PASS.
- [ ] Largest-window singleton exhaustion emits one `indeterminate/evaluation-error` record with `detail=input-too-large` before top-level remediation.
- [ ] The separate opt-in prerequisite fidelity evaluation satisfies the packed-versus-singleton recall, false-acceptance, coverage, and attribution thresholds.

## Verification
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py`.
- Run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/interfaces/contracts/test_plan_review_prerequisite_isolation.py tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`.
- Run the new opt-in prerequisite packing evaluation documented by `fidelity_spot_eval.py` and record its result in a ticket comment.
- Run `make check` and `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -m "not integration"`.
